title: excoecaria emarginata
intro: Excoecaria emarginata, also known as the tree of death or poison tree, is a tropical plant native to the coastal regions of Australia and Southeast Asia, known for its toxic properties. The plant contains a toxic compound called phorbol ester, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The medicinal properties of Excoecaria emarginata have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tinctures and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, but caution is advised due to its toxic nature.
